reply_text: It's not about aiming to get him fervor or special consideration for the atrocities he committed. In no way, shape, or form does this show paint him as a victim, despite the very real fact that he was a victim of schizophrenic bipolar and antisocial personality. It's providing relevant reasons as to why he ended up the way he did. Do not mistake making excuses, which this show isn't, for merely providing possible reasons for what lead to him doing what he did. It doesn't glorify it in any way.

video_title: MONSTER: The Ed Gein Story | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: You do have a responsibility to the victims and I think they fulfilled that.. nobody knew them a couple of months ago. Now everyone knows. That's bringing awareness, plus the victims and even their own kids, are more then likely passed away for some time now. There's a fine line between accurate representation and sugar coating something as to not offend people. What Ed Gein did wasn't nice. It was horrible. Atrocious. You can't sugar coat that or downplay the horror.. that imho would be disrespectful to the victims. So I guess it's just personal perspective.
